docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
# Credentials are read from PB_CLIENT_ID / PB_CLIENT_SECRET / PB_TSG_ID; the
# provider mints its own OAuth2 token (no token argument). The endpoint and
# auth_url are baked in at build time (production by default; staging is a
# separate build), so there is nothing to configure here.
provider "pb" {}

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• client_id (String) Service-account client id (or set PB_CLIENT_ID).
  • client_secret (String, Sensitive) Service-account client secret (or set PB_CLIENT_SECRET).
  • tsg_id (String) Tenant Service Group id, sent as scope tsg_id: (or set PB_TSG_ID).

docs.title: Nested Schema for attributes
docs.text:
Read-Only:

  • browser_brand (Attributes) Check if the device has specific browser brands and versions installed (see below for nested schema)
  • browser_eol (Attributes) Check if the device has browser versions that are end-of-life (see below for nested schema)
  • client_certificate (Attributes) Check if the device's client certificate is signed by the provided issuer certificate (see below for nested schema)
  • cs_zta_score (Attributes) Check if the device meets minimum CrowdStrike Zero Trust Assessment score requirements. If multiple score types are provided (basicScore, overallScore, breakdownScores), the latest one will be used. (see below for nested schema)
  • device_management (Attributes) Check if the device is managed by specific device management systems (e.g., Microsoft Intune, Jamf, Active Directory) (see below for nested schema)
  • device_manufacturer (Attributes) Check if the device is from specific manufacturers (e.g., Dell, HP, Lenovo) (see below for nested schema)
  • device_type (Attributes) Check if the device matches specific device types (e.g., desktop, laptop, virtual machine) (see below for nested schema)
  • disk_encryption (Attributes) Check if the device has disk encryption software installed and running (see below for nested schema)
  • endpoint_protection (Attributes) Check if the device has endpoint protection software installed and running (see below for nested schema)
  • file_existence (Attributes) Check if the device has all of the specified files present (see below for nested schema)
  • firewall (Attributes) Check if the device has firewall protection installed and running (see below for nested schema)
  • location_services (Attributes) Check if the device's location services can be accessed by Prisma Browser (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_device_management (Attributes) Check if the mobile device is managed by specific mobile device management systems (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_device_manufacturers (Attributes) Check if the mobile device is from specific manufacturers (e.g., Apple, Samsung) (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_device_type (Attributes) Check if the mobile device matches specific types (e.g., phone, tablet) (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_os_version (Attributes) Check if the mobile device is running a specific iOS or Android version (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_root_jail_break_status (Attributes) Check if the mobile device has been rooted (Android) or jailbroken (iOS) (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_screen_lock (Attributes) Check if the mobile device has screen lock protection enabled (see below for nested schema)
  • normal_osboot_mode (Attributes) Check if a device is running in OS normal boot mode (not safe mode, recovery mode or a pre-installation environment) (see below for nested schema)
  • os_password (Attributes) Check if the device has an OS authentication password configured with specific requirements (see below for nested schema)
  • os_version (Attributes) Check if the device is running a specific operating system version (see below for nested schema)
  • privileged_process (Attributes) Include only devices on which Prisma Browser is running with elevated/root permissions (see below for nested schema)
  • registry (Attributes) Check if the device has all of the specified registry key configurations (Windows only) (see below for nested schema)
  • remote_connection (Attributes) Check if the device has an active remote connection (RDP, Citrix ICA, etc.) (see below for nested schema)
  • running_processes (Attributes) Check if the device has all of the specified processes running (see below for nested schema)
  • screen_lock (Attributes) Check if the device has automatic screen lock enabled (see below for nested schema)
  • serial_number (Attributes) Check if the device's serial number is included in the provided list (see below for nested schema)
  • system_integrity (Attributes) Check if the device has advanced system integrity protection enabled (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "pb_access_and_data_rule" "example" {
  name = "Restrict uploads to sanctioned apps"
  mode = "active"

  applications = {
    # Application scope for the rule (see the Schema section below).
  }
  access = {
    # Access control configuration (see the Schema section below).
  }
  tracking = {
    # Tracking / logging configuration (see the Schema section below).
  }
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• access (Attributes) Access control configuration for an access-and-data rule.Note: on PATCH, the access block uses replace semantics — clients must re-send the full object on every update. Any field omitted is cleared (with the exception of internal fields not exposed by this API, such as authMethod and enforceRedirect, which are preserved from the existing rule). (see below for nested schema)
  • applications (Attributes) Applications scope for creating an access-and-data rule, categorized by application type. (see below for nested schema)
  • mode (String) The mode of the rule.
  • name (String) The name or title of the rule.
  • tracking (Attributes) Tracking and logging configuration for the rule (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• configuration_version (String) Configuration version to retrieve (draft, active, or version number)
  • data_controls (Attributes) Data controls for access-and-data rules.Each data control supports a specific set of data-profile content types (no profile, on-device scanning, or cloud-assisted scanning). When a rule links a data profile (an EDLP dlpProfileId) or uses inline content, controls that are not supported for the resulting content type are rejected with HTTP 400. Set bypassDlpProfileValidation: true to suppress content-agnostic compatibility rejections only — those controls save successfully but become runtime no-ops. Controls that are entirely unsupported for the resulting content type are always rejected, even when bypass is enabled. (see below for nested schema)
  • description (String) The detailed description of the rule.
  • login_controls (Attributes) Login controls for access-and-data rules. (see below for nested schema)
  • positioning (Attributes) Optional placement directive for the entity. On create, when omitted, the entity is placed at the top of the stack. On update, when omitted, the entity's current position is preserved. (see below for nested schema)
  • scope (Attributes) Provide scope to describe this rule (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• control_set (Attributes) Reference to the shared control set (policy profile) this rule's controls are inherited from. Present only when the rule links a shared control set. (see below for nested schema)
  • id (String) The unique identifier of the created resource (PULID, 29 chars).
  • priority (Number) Order position of the rule in the list
  • section (Attributes) Section this rule belongs to (null if standalone) (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for access
docs.text:
Required:

  • action (String) Top-level web-access decision.

Optional:

  • address_bar_label (String) Label shown in the browser address bar for matched pages.
  • authentication_duration_minutes (String) Duration in minutes for a timeframe window (e.g. user-prompt bypass window or authentication session validity). Must be sent as a string; integer values are rejected with a 400. Allowed values: 0 (single use / every time), 10 (10m), 60 (1h), 240 (4h), 540 (9h), 720 (12h), 1440 (24h), 4320 (3d), 10080 (7d), 20160 (14d), 43200 (30d), 86400 (60d), 129600 (90d).
  • custom_dialog_text (Attributes) Custom dialog text shown to the user. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_label (Attributes) Custom address-bar label configuration. Required when addressBarLabel is 'custom'. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for data_controls
docs.text:
Optional:

  • bypass_dlp_profile_validation (Boolean) Bypass the content-agnostic data-profile compatibility check for controls in this object. When false (default), controls incompatible with the configured dlpProfileId are rejected with HTTP 400. When true, the rule saves and content-agnostic incompatible controls become runtime no-ops. Controls entirely unsupported for the resulting content type are always rejected, even when this is true.
  • camera (Attributes) Control the ability to use camera in web pages. (see below for nested schema)
  • clipboard (Attributes) Control cut, copy and paste. At least one direction must be active: either dataIn.active or dataOut.active must be true. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_http_header (Attributes) Set a custom HTTP header to identify HTTP/S requests. (see below for nested schema)
  • developer_tools_on_web_pages (Attributes) Control DevTools to avoid potential data leaks, inspecting page content, and running unauthorized code in the console. (see below for nested schema)
  • dlp_profile (Attributes) Expanded reference to an Enterprise DLP (EDLP) data profile. (see below for nested schema)
  • dlp_profile_id (String) Enterprise DLP (EDLP) data profile id (the same value returned by EDLP GET /v2/api/data-profiles). Write-only on POST/PATCH; reads return dlpProfile instead. Send null on PATCH to unlink the rule from any EDLP profile.
  • file_protection (Attributes) Control how files enter and leave the rule's applications. Configure fileDownload and fileUpload independently — each direction owns its action, filters, prompt, MFA setting, and custom dialog text. Replaces the legacy fileDownload and fileUpload top-level controls. (see below for nested schema)
  • gen_aiprompt (Attributes) A simple control with an allow or block action. Re-usable by any data control that only needs a binary allow/block decision. (see below for nested schema)
  • live_page_scanning (Attributes) Prevent Exposure to malicious pages using real-time page scanning, powered by Prisma Browser's Advanced Web Protection. (see below for nested schema)
  • malicious_file_protection (Attributes) Prevent or detect download or upload of malicious files via Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • microphone (Attributes) Control the ability to use microphone in web pages. (see below for nested schema)
  • print (Attributes) Control printing of web pages and files opened in Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • read_only_webpage (Attributes) (see below for nested schema)
  • screenshot (Attributes) Control screen capture (using snipping tools or "print screen"), screen recording, and screen sharing using video conferencing tools. (see below for nested schema)
  • typing_guard (Attributes) Monitor and prevent sensitive data as it's typed into web applications, with inline protection when possible. (see below for nested schema)
  • user_agent (Attributes) Set additional User-Agent component to the default User-Agent string to identify HTTP/S requests. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_user_interaction (Attributes) Control webpage functionality by removing or hiding selected elements from end users, ensuring only authorized actions remain available. (see below for nested schema)
  • webpage_data_masking (Attributes) (see below for nested schema)
  • webpage_watermarking (Attributes) Control webpage watermarking to deter users from taking screenshots using external means (e.g. mobile device). (see below for nested schema)

Required:

Unlike other reference ids in this API, which are internal Prisma identifiers, this value comes from the Enterprise DLP system, so it is not interchangeable with ids returned for the same profile on other Prisma surfaces. docs.title: Nested Schema for data_controls.file_protection.file_download
docs.text:
Required:

  • action (String) How downloads are handled. allow saves the file to the user's local disk normally. useInPrismaBrowserOnly saves the file encrypted so it can only be opened inside Prisma Browser. saveToOrganizationStorage routes the download to an organization-managed cloud storage location instead of the local disk. block prevents the download.

Optional:

  • allow_open_outside_browser (Boolean) Hidden from the published OpenAPI doc but present in the underlying schema — the gateway accepts the field on POST/PUT and returns it on GET. When true, users can also open the protected file in external apps on their device. When false, the file can only be opened inside Prisma Browser.
  • apply_on (String) Whether the download rule applies to every file (any) or only to files that match the filters below (specific).
  • before_download (Attributes) Prompt and MFA settings applied before the action is enforced. (see below for nested schema)
  • cloud_storage_provider (String) The cloud-storage integration (OneDrive, Google Drive, Box, etc.) that downloads are routed to. Required when action is saveToOrganizationStorage; rejected for other actions. POST/PUT send only the ID; GET responses include both the ID and a sibling cloudStorageProviderName with the resolved display name.
  • cloud_storage_provider_name (String) Response-only. The resolved display name of the cloud-storage integration referenced by cloudStorageProvider. Omitted when the integration has been deleted or cannot be resolved.
  • custom_dialog_text____ (Attributes) Custom dialog text shown to the user. (see below for nested schema)
  • file_sha256filter (Attributes) Filter by SHA-256 file hashes. (see below for nested schema)
  • file_size_limit (Attributes) Filter by file size threshold. (see below for nested schema)
  • file_type_filter (Attributes) Filter by file type — categories, sub-categories, or specific extensions. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for data_controls.file_protection.file_download.file_sha256filter

Required:

docs.title: Nested Schema for data_controls.file_protection.file_download.file_size_limit
docs.text:
Required:

  • value (Number) The size threshold, expressed in the chosen unit. On read, value and unit are normalized to the largest unit that yields an exact integer (e.g. 1024 B is returned as 1 KB).

Optional:

  • unit (String) Unit for value — bytes, kilobytes, megabytes, or gigabytes.

docs.title: Nested Schema for data_controls.file_protection.file_download.file_type_filter
docs.text:
Required:

  • operator (String) Whether the rule applies to files in the combined list of predefinedTypes + customExtensions (in), or to all files except those (notIn).

Optional:

  • custom_extensions (List of String) Specific file extensions — both predefined extensions from the Prisma Browser catalog (e.g., pdf, docx) and your own proprietary or rare formats (e.g., xyz). All extensions live in this single array regardless of catalog membership.
  • predefined_types (List of String) Broad file-type buckets from the Prisma Browser catalog. Either a category (e.g., documents) or a sub-category (e.g., excel). Use this when the rule should match a whole group of files; for specific extensions, use customExtensions instead.

docs.title: Nested Schema for data_controls.file_protection.file_upload
docs.text:
Required:

  • action (String) How uploads are handled. allow lets any file be uploaded to the rule's apps. allowProtectedBetweenRuleApps lets a user upload only Prisma-protected files that were previously downloaded from one of this rule's web applications — keeping protected content moving only between apps that share the same scope. Requires fileDownload.action=useInPrismaBrowserOnly on a matching rule. blockOnlyProtected is the inverse — ordinary files upload normally but protected files are blocked. block prevents all uploads. allowProtectedBetweenRuleApps is a legacy, read-only value: it may be returned for pre-existing rules but is not accepted when creating or updating a rule, and such requests are rejected.

Optional:

  • apply_on (String) Whether the upload rule applies to every file (any) or only to files that match the filters below (specific).
  • before_upload (Attributes) Prompt and MFA settings applied before the action is enforced. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_dialog_text_____ (Attributes) Custom dialog text shown to the user. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "pb_cloud_storage" "example" {
  display_name = "Corporate object store"
  enabled      = true
  type_        = "s3"
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• display_name (String) Human-readable name for the provider. Must be unique.
  • type_ (String) The cloud storage provider type.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• google (Attributes) Request body to create a Google Drive provider. (see below for nested schema)
  • microsoft (Attributes) Request body to create a Microsoft OneDrive provider. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) Unique identifier for the provider.
  • status (String) Live connectivity status of the cloud storage provider.

docs.title: Nested Schema for google
docs.text:
Required:

  • email_address (String) Google Workspace admin email for domain-wide delegation.
  • service_account_key (String) Base64-encoded Google service account JSON key.

docs.text:
Required:

  • client_id (String) Azure AD application (client) ID.
  • client_secret (String) Azure AD application client secret.
  • tenant_id (String) Azure AD tenant ID.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "pb_customization_rule" "example" {
  name = "Block incognito browsing"
  mode = "active"

  controls = {
    incognito = {
      action = "block"
    }
  }

  # scope is optional. Omit it (or set is_any = true) to apply to everyone.
  scope = {
    users = {
      is_any = true
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• controls (Attributes) Controls for customization rules. (see below for nested schema)
  • mode (String) The mode of the rule. The 'monitor' mode is not supported for this rule type.
  • name (String) The name or title of the rule.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• configuration_version (String) Configuration version to retrieve (draft, active, or version number)
  • description (String) The detailed description of the rule.
  • positioning (Attributes) Optional placement directive for the entity. On create, when omitted, the entity is placed at the top of the stack. On update, when omitted, the entity's current position is preserved. (see below for nested schema)
  • scope (Attributes) Provide scope to describe this rule (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) The unique identifier of the created resource (PULID, 29 chars).
  • policy_profile (Attributes) The linked policy profile (see below for nested schema)
  • priority (Number) Order position of the rule in the list
  • section (Attributes) Section this rule belongs to (null if standalone) (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for controls
docs.text:
Optional:

  • admin_messages (Attributes) Control whether admin messages appear on the new tab page. (see below for nested schema)
  • auto_launch_external_applications (Attributes) Control whether websites can open external desktop apps without prompting users. (see below for nested schema)
  • automatic_client_certificate_selection (Attributes) Automatically selects matching client certificates for specified URLs to facilitate seamless authentication. (see below for nested schema)
  • background_image (Attributes) Set new tab page background (see below for nested schema)
  • brand_color (Attributes) Choose a brand color to be applied across all user-facing browser elements, including dialogs, block pages, and other elements (see below for nested schema)
  • browser_icon (Attributes) Set the icon for the browser in the operating system (see below for nested schema)
  • built_in_dns_client (Attributes) Choose whether DNS is resolved by the browsers built-in client or by the operating system. (see below for nested schema)
  • cached_session_duration (Attributes) Set the duration the browser can run using securely cached credentials without syncing to the Prisma Browser management console. (see below for nested schema)
  • company_logo (Attributes) Set the logo displayed in the browser on dialogs, homepage, and other pages. (see below for nested schema)
  • company_name (Attributes) Set the name displayed in the browser on dialogs, homepage, and other pages. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_browser_preferences (Attributes) Apply custom browser preferences. Please contact Prisma Browser's support team for further guidance. When mode is enabled, preferences is required; when mode is disabled, preferences must not be set. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_http_header (Attributes) Set a custom HTTP header to identify HTTP/S requests. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_notice (Attributes) Set a customized notice that will be displayed to end-users when they use the Prisma mobile browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_profile_preferences (Attributes) Apply custom browser profile preferences. Please contact Prisma Browser's support team for further guidance. When mode is enabled, preferences is required; when mode is disabled, preferences must not be set. (see below for nested schema)
  • custom_texts (Attributes) Customize and edit texts shown in browser dialogs and block pages. (see below for nested schema)
  • default_ai_provider (Attributes) Selected provider will appear as a "Prompt AI" shortcut in the New Tab Page Enterprise Omnibox. (see below for nested schema)
  • default_search_provider (Attributes) Control the default search engine provider for the address bar and context menu search access. (see below for nested schema)
  • deployment_upgrade (Attributes) Select Prisma Browser upgrade channel. (see below for nested schema)
  • desktop_sharing (Attributes) Control end-user ability to share their entire desktop screen or desktop apps during video conferencing sessions via web apps such as Zoom, MS Teams, and Slack. (see below for nested schema)
  • extension_force_install (Attributes) Set specific extensions to be installed automatically in Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • force_page_refresh_on_policy_update (Attributes) Force a page refresh while the end-user is browsing to immediately enforce policy changes for certain controls. (see below for nested schema)
  • history_collection (Attributes) Collect new users' browsing history to identify trends and support enforcement decisions based on historical data. (see below for nested schema)
  • home_page (Attributes) Set the target page when a user clicks the Home Page button. (see below for nested schema)
  • identity_provider_synced_shortcuts (Attributes) Display a user's IDP synced company assigned applications as shortcuts on their New Tab page. (see below for nested schema)
  • incognito (Attributes) Control the ability to open Prisma Browser in Incognito mode. (see below for nested schema)
  • internal_network_detection (Attributes) Configure internal network detection to ensure traffic is routed in an optimal way. (see below for nested schema)
  • managed_shortcuts (Attributes) Set the applications that appear on the new tab page and in bookmarks. (see below for nested schema)
  • microsoft_auto_sso (Attributes) Enable automatic web-application user sign-in for Microsoft Entra ID accounts, based on the device signed-in account. (see below for nested schema)
  • new_tab_page (Attributes) Set the type of page when a new Prisma Browser or tab is opened. (see below for nested schema)
  • on_browser_startup (Attributes) Control the browser's 'On Startup' settings (see below for nested schema)
  • onboarding_wizard (Attributes) Configure the end-user onboarding wizard. (see below for nested schema)
  • page_translation_google_translate (Attributes) Control the Google Translation banner on foreign-language pages. (see below for nested schema)
  • prisma_browser_desktop_extender (Attributes) Control the use of Prisma Browser Local Extender (PABL). (see below for nested schema)
  • profile_sync (Attributes) Set up user profile sync across all the devices a user is logged in to. (see below for nested schema)
  • proxy_auto_configuration_pac_file (Attributes) Configure Prisma Browser to use a proxy auto-configuration (PAC) file. (see below for nested schema)
  • real_user_monitoring (Attributes) Control the use of RUM within Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • request_desktop_mobile_site (Attributes) Override default site settings for specific domains on mobile devices. (see below for nested schema)
  • search_engine_content_filtering (Attributes) Enable content filtering for search engines to reduce exposure to inappropriate or distracting material (e.g., adult or violent content). This setting is supported by selected search engines, as appears in the list below. (see below for nested schema)
  • search_suggestions (Attributes) Show live search suggestions in the address bar as users type. (see below for nested schema)
  • suggest_default_browser (Attributes) Controls whether Prisma Browser suggests itself as the default browser to the user. (see below for nested schema)
  • theme_color (Attributes) Set Prisma Browser's theme color. (see below for nested schema)
  • traffic_flow (Attributes) Choose the way the Prisma Browser handles network traffic. (see below for nested schema)
  • user_agent (Attributes) Set additional User-Agent component to the default User-Agent string to identify HTTP/S requests. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for controls.deployment_upgrade
docs.text:
Required:

  • mode (String) Prisma Browser upgrade channel

Optional:

  • expedite_critical_security_updates (Boolean) Whether critical security updates can bypass the configured delay. Applies when mode is default and versionUpdateDelayDays > 0, or mode is lts and patchDelayDays > 0. Defaults to true when applicable. Must not be set when the relevant delay is 0, or when mode is pinned or noUpdates.
  • force_rollback (Attributes) Whether to force rollback when the pinned version is older than latest. Applies when mode is pinned. Defaults to false for both platforms when omitted. (see below for nested schema)
  • grace_period_hours (Number) Time in hours users can defer the forced update relaunch. Applies when mode is default, lts, or pinned. Defaults to 4 when omitted. Must not be set when mode is noUpdates.
  • major_release_delay_days (Number) Delay in days before receiving the next available LTS major release. Applies when mode is lts.
  • patch_delay_days (Number) Delay in days before receiving LTS security and bug patches. Applies when mode is lts. Must be less than or equal to majorReleaseDelayDays.
  • pinned_versions (Attributes) Pinned target versions by desktop OS. Required when mode is pinned. Must not be set for other modes. (see below for nested schema)
  • version_update_delay_days (Number) Delay in days before receiving the latest default-channel version. Applies when mode is default.

Accepts a hostname/domain (optionally with "." or "" wildcards, including a bare "" catch-all for all sites), or a "scheme://host[:port]/path" match pattern. resource "pb_device_group" "example" {
  name     = "Corporate macOS"
  platform = "macos"
}

docs.title: Required

  • platform (String) Device group platform

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• attributes (Attributes) (see below for nested schema)
  • configuration_version (String) Configuration version to retrieve (draft, active, or version number)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• created_at (String) Created at timestamp
  • created_by (String) Created by user
  • device_group_id (String, Deprecated) Deprecated: use id instead. The ID of the newly created device group.
  • devices (List of String) Device IDs in this group
  • id (String) The ID of the newly created device group
  • updated_at (String) Updated at timestamp
  • updated_by (String) Updated by user

docs.title: Nested Schema for attributes
docs.text:
Optional:

  • browser_brand (Attributes) Check if the device has specific browser brands and versions installed (see below for nested schema)
  • browser_eol (Attributes) Check if the device has browser versions that are end-of-life (see below for nested schema)
  • client_certificate (Attributes) Check if the device's client certificate is signed by the provided issuer certificate (see below for nested schema)
  • cs_zta_score (Attributes) Check if the device meets minimum CrowdStrike Zero Trust Assessment score requirements. If multiple score types are provided (basicScore, overallScore, breakdownScores), the latest one will be used. (see below for nested schema)
  • device_management (Attributes) Check if the device is managed by specific device management systems (e.g., Microsoft Intune, Jamf, Active Directory) (see below for nested schema)
  • device_manufacturer (Attributes) Check if the device is from specific manufacturers (e.g., Dell, HP, Lenovo) (see below for nested schema)
  • device_type (Attributes) Check if the device matches specific device types (e.g., desktop, laptop, virtual machine) (see below for nested schema)
  • disk_encryption (Attributes) Check if the device has disk encryption software installed and running (see below for nested schema)
  • endpoint_protection (Attributes) Check if the device has endpoint protection software installed and running (see below for nested schema)
  • file_existence (Attributes) Check if the device has all of the specified files present (see below for nested schema)
  • firewall (Attributes) Check if the device has firewall protection installed and running (see below for nested schema)
  • location_services (Attributes) Check if the device's location services can be accessed by Prisma Browser (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_device_management (Attributes) Check if the mobile device is managed by specific mobile device management systems (see below for nested schema)

  • privileged_process (Attributes) Include only devices on which Prisma Browser is running with elevated/root permissions (see below for nested schema)

  • remote_connection (Attributes) Check if the device has an active remote connection (RDP, Citrix ICA, etc.) (see below for nested schema)
  • running_processes (Attributes) Check if the device has all of the specified processes running (see below for nested schema)
  • screen_lock (Attributes) Check if the device has automatic screen lock enabled (see below for nested schema)
  • serial_number (Attributes) Check if the device's serial number is included in the provided list (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
resource "pb_security_rule" "example" {
  name = "Default security rule"
  mode = "active"

  controls = {
    # Security controls for the rule (see the Schema section below).
  }
}

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• controls (Attributes) Controls for security rules. (see below for nested schema)
  • mode (String) The mode of the rule. The 'monitor' mode is not supported for this rule type.
  • name (String) The name or title of the rule.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• configuration_version (String) Configuration version to retrieve (draft, active, or version number)
  • description (String) The detailed description of the rule.
  • positioning (Attributes) Optional placement directive for the entity. On create, when omitted, the entity is placed at the top of the stack. On update, when omitted, the entity's current position is preserved. (see below for nested schema)
  • scope (Attributes) Provide scope to describe this rule (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Read-Only
docs.text:
• id (String) The unique identifier of the created resource (PULID, 29 chars).
  • policy_profile (Attributes) The linked policy profile (see below for nested schema)
  • priority (Number) Order position of the rule in the list
  • section (Attributes) Section this rule belongs to (null if standalone) (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for controls
docs.text:
Optional:

  • advanced_browser_protection (Attributes) Stops zero-day WebAssembly attacks in real time by monitoring in-browser memory behavior and terminating malicious execution before any impact occurs. (see below for nested schema)
  • allowed_or_blocked_extensions (Attributes) Control browser extension availability to users based on extension ID or risk score. (see below for nested schema)
  • allowed_printers (Attributes) Control which printers can be used when printing from Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • authentication_server_allowlist (Attributes) List the servers allowed to use Integrated Authentication. (see below for nested schema)
  • autofill_of_credit_cards (Attributes) Control saving credit cards in the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • autofill_of_forms (Attributes) Control saving forms data in the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • basic_authentication_over_http (Attributes) Set whether basic authentication can be used over HTTP websites. (see below for nested schema)
  • block_extensions_by_permissions (Attributes) Prevent users from running extensions that require certain permissions. (see below for nested schema)
  • browser_history (Attributes) Control the ability to delete history from the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • browser_lock (Attributes) Require the user to unlock their browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • browser_self_protection (Attributes) Enables a kernel-mode driver that provides advanced runtime security for the browser. This protection is available only on Windows and applies only to devices where Prisma Browser is installed with admin permissions and the user is running the browser as admin. (see below for nested schema)
  • cast (Attributes) Control the ability to cast a tab or the whole desktop via Prisma Browser. Prisma Browser restart required to apply. (see below for nested schema)
  • concurrent_number_of_devices (Attributes) Control the maximum number of devices users can be logged into at the same time. Which fields are required or allowed depends on action and limitMode (see individual field descriptions). (see below for nested schema)
  • cookies (Attributes) Control the ability to store cookies on the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • cookies_protection (Attributes) Control the encryption of stored cookies on the device. (see below for nested schema)
  • developer_tools_for_extensions (Attributes) Control DevTools to avoid inspection of browser extensions. (see below for nested schema)
  • dns_over_https (Attributes) Set DNS resolving on top of the HTTPS protocol, for encrypting the requests and their resolutions. resolverUrl and adnsEnabled=true are mutually exclusive. (see below for nested schema)
  • end_process_via_task_manager (Attributes) Choose whether end users can end browser processes via the browser task manager. (see below for nested schema)
  • enhanced_tracking_protection (Attributes) Manage tracking protection and cross-site tracking. (see below for nested schema)
  • file_system_api (Attributes) Control the use of the File System API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • flush_browser_data (Attributes) Set temporary browser sessions, so browser data will be cleaned upon close or time period. (see below for nested schema)
  • force_https (Attributes) Force using HTTPS instead of HTTP to reduce the risk of MitM attacks and sending sensitive information in cleartext. (see below for nested schema)
  • google_cloud_print (Attributes) Control the use of the Google Cloud Print, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • hide_sensitive_data_from_extensions (Attributes) Prevent all extensions from accessing cookies and authorization data sent by the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • internet_explorer_compatibility_mode (Attributes) Allow access to legacy websites using Internet Explorer compatibility mode. (see below for nested schema)
  • java_script_running_from_omnibox (Attributes) Control the ability to execute JavaScript code from the browser omnibox. (see below for nested schema)
  • java_script_v8jit_and_web_assembly (Attributes) Block JavaScript v8 JIT to reduce exploitation risks and to activate vulnerability mitigation techniques. Block WebAssembly (WASM) to reduce exploitation risks. (see below for nested schema)
  • kerberos_delegation_allowlist (Attributes) List the hosts that may forward a user's Kerberos ticket to downstream services. (see below for nested schema)
  • keylogging_protection (Attributes) Prevent keyloggers from capturing user input while using the browser (Windows only). (see below for nested schema)
  • launching_external_applications (Attributes) Configure the default behavior when users click links that launch external applications. You can then define exceptions for specific applications. (see below for nested schema)
  • legacy_password_manager (Attributes) Enable the Prisma Access Browser legacy password manager for managing and securing company passwords and secrets. (see below for nested schema)
  • local_fonts (Attributes) Control the access to local fonts installed on the device, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • local_network_access_restrictions (Attributes) Manage website access to local network endpoints. (see below for nested schema)
  • mobile_password_saving (Attributes) Control passwords saving in the mobile browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • native_messaging_hosts (Attributes) Control which native messaging hosts can communicate with Prisma Browser and its extensions. (see below for nested schema)
  • notifications (Attributes) Control the ability to display notifications in the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • open_links_in_external_apps (Attributes) Control the ability of other apps to open links from Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • pages_with_insecure_content (Attributes) Set whether pages with insecure content are available. (see below for nested schema)
  • pages_with_ssl_errors (Attributes) Block "Proceed to [FQDN] (unsafe)" option when getting an SSL error. Such errors might appear during SSL MitM attack. (see below for nested schema)
  • password_manager (Attributes) Enable enterprise password management powered by Idira. (see below for nested schema)
  • pdfium (Attributes) Control the use of the PDFium library to render PDF pages, which might be exploited. When PDFium is disabled, encrypted PDF files won't be loaded in Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • popups (Attributes) Control the ability to display popups in the browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • post_quantum_key_security (Attributes) Control whether to offer a post-quantum key agreement algorithm in TLS. (see below for nested schema)
  • print_preview (Attributes) Control the preview in the printing window, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • quic_protocol (Attributes) Control the use of the QUIC protocol, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • remote_debugging (Attributes) Control remote debugging on Android and iOS devices to prevent data leaks and simplify troubleshooting. (see below for nested schema)
  • remote_host_firewall_traversal (Attributes) Manage whether Remote Desktop may connect from outside the LAN or VPN by traversing firewalls. (see below for nested schema)
  • restrict_extension_host_permissions (Attributes) Prevent extensions from running scripts and accessing content in websites. (see below for nested schema)
  • sensors_api (Attributes) Control the use of the Sensor API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • session_refresh (Attributes) Periodically require the user to re-authenticate. (see below for nested schema)
  • strict_origin_isolation (Attributes) Isolates every origin in its own agent-cluster, preventing scripts on one sub-domain from interacting with another. (see below for nested schema)
  • third_party_cookies (Attributes) Set whether elements in the web page that aren't from the domain that's in the browser's address bar can set cookies. (see below for nested schema)
  • trusted_certificate_authorities (Attributes) Choose the certificate authorities trusted by Prisma Browser. (see below for nested schema)
  • user_data_directory_protection (Attributes) Enforce hardware based detections to prevent data directory and browser session theft. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_bluetooth_api (Attributes) Control the use of the WebBluetooth API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_clipboard_api (Attributes) Control the use of the Clipboard API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_gl_api (Attributes) Control the use of the WebGL library, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_hid_api (Attributes) Control the availability of the WebHID API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_rtc (Attributes) Control the use of WebRTC mechanism, which might be exploited. Disabling it might impact the usability of websites working with WebRTC functionalities, like call/video streaming. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_serial_api (Attributes) Control the use of the WebSerial API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)
  • web_usb_api (Attributes) Control the use of the WebUSB API, which might be exploited. (see below for nested schema)

docs.title: Nested Schema for controls.concurrent_number_of_devices
docs.text:
Required:

  • action (String) Whether to limit the number of concurrent devices. When 'unlimited', no other fields may be set.

Optional:

  • limit_mode (String) Whether the device limit applies to all devices combined or per device type. Required when action is 'limited'. Must not be set when action is 'unlimited'.
  • max_desktop_devices (Number) Maximum number of desktop devices (macOS/Windows/Linux). Use null for no limit, or 0 to block desktop devices entirely. Required when limitMode is 'byDeviceType'. Must not be set when limitMode is 'byTotalDevices' or when action is 'unlimited'. maxDesktopDevices and maxMobileDevices cannot both be null, and cannot both be 0.
  • max_mobile_devices (Number) Maximum number of mobile devices (iOS/Android). Use null for no limit, or 0 to block mobile devices entirely. Required when limitMode is 'byDeviceType'. Must not be set when limitMode is 'byTotalDevices' or when action is 'unlimited'. maxDesktopDevices and maxMobileDevices cannot both be null, and cannot both be 0.
  • max_total_devices (Number) Maximum total number of devices a user can be logged into across all device types. Required when limitMode is 'byTotalDevices'. Must not be set when limitMode is 'byDeviceType' or when action is 'unlimited'.
